sourceMovie.title: Arkansas
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Arkansas (2020) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Clark Duke's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Arkansas clicked for you, A Walk Among the Tombstones (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Arkansas?
faq.answer: Try A Walk Among the Tombstones, Mississippi Burning, Street Kings—each shares DNA with Arkansas while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Arkansas?
faq.answer: Arkansas (2020) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Action, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Arkansas worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
